chunks.title: Components & Initial Setup
chunks.content:
## Components & Initial Setup

**Components**
- 58 cards
- 6 game aid cards (one per player)
- Rulebook

**Setup Steps**
1. Shuffle all 58 cards into a face-down draw deck.
2. Flip the top 2 cards face-up, placed side by side as **2 separate discard piles**.
3. All players start with **no cards in hand**.
4. Choose a start player randomly.
5. Each player takes 1 game aid card.

**VP Thresholds (game end)**

| Players | Points to Win |
|---------|---------------|
| 2 | 40 |
| 3 | 35 |
| 4 | 30 |

chunks.title: Round Reset
chunks.content:
## Round Reset

After each round is scored:
1. Gather **all** cards, shuffle, form a new draw deck.
2. Flip the top 2 cards face-up to start 2 fresh discard piles.
3. Players start the new round with **no cards in hand**.

**First player next round:**
- **LAST CHANCE** triggered → the player who called it goes first.
- **STOP** called → the next player **clockwise** from the STOP-caller goes first.

chunks.title: Turn Actions
chunks.content:
## Turn Actions

On your turn, perform **exactly one** of the two actions below, then optionally play Duos.

| Action | What You Do |
|---|---|
| **Draw from Deck** | Take top 2 cards; keep 1 in hand, place the other face-up on top of either discard pile |
| **Take from Discard** | Take the top card from either discard pile into your hand |

### After Your Action — Play Duos (Optional)
- A **Duo** = 2 matching cards played face-up in front of you
- Triggers the pair's special effect and scores **1 point** at round end
- You may play **multiple Duos** in the same turn
- You may also keep matched pairs in hand — they still score **1 pt per pair** at round end

chunks.title: End-of-Round Triggers
chunks.content:
## End-of-Round Triggers

| Trigger | Condition | What Happens |
|---|---|---|
| **STOP or LAST CHANCE** | After your action, if your visible total (hand + played Duos) is **7 or more points** | You may announce to end the round |
| **Deck Exhaustion** | Draw deck runs out | Round ends immediately as if STOP was called |

- **STOP** — round ends immediately; **all players** reveal and score normally.
- **LAST CHANCE** — all opponents each take one more turn, then scoring happens (see STOP vs LAST CHANCE in Scoring).

chunks.title: Duo Cards — Pairs & Immediate Effects
chunks.content:
## Duo Cards

Play **both cards face-up** to trigger the effect. Each duo scores **+1 pt** at round end.

| Pair | Effect |
|---|---|
| Crab + Crab | No effect — just 1 pt for the pair |
| Fish + Fish | Draw the top card of the deck |
| Seahorse + Seahorse | Look at top 5 cards of deck; take 1, shuffle rest back |
| Crab + Lobster | Take top 5 cards of deck; keep 1, shuffle other 4 back |
| Penguin + Penguin | Look at **all** opponents' hands |
| Boat + Boat | Steal 1 card from an opponent's hand (**opponent** chooses which) |
| Shark + Swimmer | Steal 1 card of **your choice** from an opponent's hand |

> **Note:** Crab has 9 cards; any 2 crabs form a pair. Penguin has 3 cards; any 2 penguins form a pair.

chunks.title: Collector & Scoring Cards — Point Values
chunks.content:
## Collector Cards (scored by quantity held at round end)

| Card | Copies | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Shell | ×6 | 0 | 2 | 4 | 6 | 8 | 10 |
| Octopus | ×5 | 0 | 3 | 6 | 9 | 12 | — |
| Penguin | ×3 | 1 | 3 | 5 | — | — | — |
| Sailor | ×2 | 0 | 5 | — | — | — | — |

## Special Scoring Cards

| Card | Copies | Scoring Rule |
|---|---|---|
| Mermaid | ×4 | +1 pt per card of your **most-common colour** (each Mermaid targets a different colour; can't double-count). Hold **all 4** → **instant game win**. |
| Lighthouse | ×1 | +1 pt per **Boat** card you hold |
| Shoal of Fish | ×1 | +1 pt per **Fish** card you hold |
| Seahorse | — | Also counts as 1 collector card of your **largest collection** type (requires ≥1 of that type) |

## Colour Bonus (every player, every round)

+1 pt per card of your most-common colour (hand + played area). **Mermaid cards are excluded** from this tally.

chunks.title: Expansion Cards (More)
chunks.content:
## Sea Salt & Paper — *More* Expansion

The **More** expansion (published separately by Bombyx) adds new card types to the base game. Specific card effects and scoring rules for expansion cards were **not present** in the base rulebook used for this reference.

Consult the *More* expansion rulebook insert for details on new card types and any rule modifications.

chunks.title: End-Round Scoring — All VP Sources
chunks.content:
## End-Round Scoring

Score **all cards in hand + face-up Duos** each round.

### Set Scoring

| Card | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Shell | 0 | 2 | 4 | 6 | 8 | 10 |
| Octopus | 0 | 3 | 6 | 9 | 12 | — |
| Penguin | 1 | 3 | 5 | — | — | — |
| Sailor pair | 0 | 5 | — | — | — | — |

### Individual Card Scoring

- **Duo pairs** (played face-up OR matching pair in hand): 1 pt each
- **Mermaid**: 1 pt per card of your most-common colour (each Mermaid counts a *different* colour; no repeats)
- **Lighthouse**: 1 pt per Boat card held
- **Shoal of Fish**: 1 pt per Fish card held

### Colour Bonus (all players, every round)

1 pt per card of your most-common colour — Mermaids **not** counted

### Game End & Thresholds

| Players | Points to trigger end |
|---|---|
| 2 | 40 |
| 3 | 35 |
| 4 | 30 |

When ≥1 player hits the threshold, **highest cumulative total wins**.

**Tiebreaker:** tied player who did *not* trigger the final round wins. If still tied, share victory.

**Instant Win:** collect all 4 Mermaids in one round → win immediately regardless of scores.

chunks.title: STOP vs LAST CHANCE — Scoring Consequences
chunks.content:
## STOP vs LAST CHANCE Scoring

### STOP

Round ends **immediately**. All players (including the triggerer) reveal and score **full points + colour bonus** normally.

Next round: play passes to the next player clockwise from the STOP-caller.

### LAST CHANCE

All opponents each take **one more turn**, then compare totals:

| Outcome | Triggerer scores | Opponents score |
|---|---|---|
| Triggerer has **≥ points** than every opponent | Full points + colour bonus | Colour bonus only |
| Any opponent has **more points** than triggerer | Colour bonus only | Full points + colour bonus |

> Colour bonus = 1 pt per card of your most-common colour (Mermaids not counted).

chunks.title: Strategic Tips for New Players
chunks.content:
## Strategic Tips for New Players

### Card Management
- **Play Duos early for effects, not just points.** A Duo played face-up triggers its effect immediately — Crab+Lobster or Seahorse+Seahorse let you dig into the deck and grab exactly what you need. Hoarding Duos in hand only earns 1 pt each, which is rarely better.
- **Watch the face-up discard piles.** Action 2 lets you take the top card of any discard pile directly — no random draw. If an opponent just discarded something you need for a Duo or set, take it before someone else does.
- **Build toward one colour intentionally.** The colour bonus (1 pt per card of your most-common colour) scores every round for every player. Concentrating on one colour makes your bonus meaningful and sets you up for Mermaid synergy.

### Timing the Round End
- **STOP is almost always correct when you're not sure.** Calling LAST CHANCE is a high-stakes gamble — if any opponent beats your score, you score nothing except colour bonus. Early in the game, banking points with STOP is safer than risking a zero.
- **Use LAST CHANCE only when you have a commanding lead.** Before calling it, mentally estimate opponents' likely scores. If someone has a large visible Duo tableau or a long set on the table, your lead may be smaller than it looks.
- **Don't let the deck run out on you.** A deck-empty round end is treated as STOP — fine in principle, but you lose the option to call LAST CHANCE on your own terms. If you're close to a winning score, end the round yourself rather than waiting.

### Scoring Awareness
- **Track the victory threshold for your player count.** The target drops sharply (40 → 35 → 30 pts) as players are added. In a 4-player game you can reach the threshold surprisingly fast; don't assume you need a big engine before ending a round.
- **Mermaids are a side objective, not a main plan.** Collecting all 4 Mermaids is an instant win, but opponents will block it once they notice. Treat Mermaids as bonus multipliers for your colour strategy rather than a primary goal.
